mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near the zero lower bound, additional declines signal policy accommodation rather than distress, lower the discount rate on equities, and support higher S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations do not show higher 20d forward SPX returns than the unconditional baseline, or if the signal turns negative in repeated out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE from already elevated levels indicates weakening labor income and recession risk, which lowers expected corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if rises in UNRATE are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the sign flips to positive in a stable rolling out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and HOUST captures broad household and retail demand acceleration, which raises expected sales growth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST were both available and positive in the 2012-04 snapshot
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the combined signal fails to improve 60d forward returns versus unconditional baseline across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while PAYEMS is flat, firms are extracting more output from the same labor base, which signals margin improvement and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 96.5685 and PAYEMS was 132989 on 2012-03/04 snapshot
failure_condition: Rejected if the INDPRO-up/PAYEMS-flat regime does not produce higher 20d SPX returns than other states, or if negative returns dominate within triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in CPIAUCSL inflation after prior price pressure boosts real purchasing power and reduces the probability of policy tightening, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls by at least 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Rejected if disinflation triggers are not followed by above-baseline 5d SPX gains, or if the effect disappears after accounting for rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS after labor-market stress indicates broad job creation, stronger wage income, and improving household demand, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises in each of the last 3 monthly releases
failure_condition: Rejected if three-month employment growth is not associated with positive 60d SPX returns, or if the triggered sample underperforms the unconditional mean across walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS remains weak, the market reads the slowdown as soft final demand rather than temporary inventory noise, and S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDP 2-quarter annualized growth falls below 1% and RSAFS 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Rejected if low GDP growth with weak retail sales does not coincide with negative 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined signal has no incremental power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with flat CPIAUCSL implies real policy restraint rather than benign normalization, which compresses equity multiples and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.14% and CPIAUCSL was 229.177 on 2012-04-01
failure_condition: Rejected if rate rises during tightening regimes fail to produce negative 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term does not outperform a single-variable FEDFUNDS model.
